rules.description: Every repo runs automated checks in CI.
rules.what: Flags repos with no workflow under .github/workflows.
rules.why: CI is the enforcement backstop that keeps standards from drifting.

rules.description: Version-controlled git hooks ship with the repo.
rules.what: Flags repos without a non-empty .githooks directory.
rules.why: Committed hooks make local gates reviewable and unbypassable.

rules.description: A gitignore keeps build junk and secrets out of history.
rules.what: Flags repos with no .gitignore at the root.
rules.why: One ignore file prevents accidental commits of local state.

rules.description: A README states what the repo is and how to run it.
rules.what: Flags repos with no README at the root.
rules.why: An entry-point document keeps the workflow discoverable.

rules.description: Workflow files never carry hardcoded secrets.
rules.what: Flags private keys, AWS keys, or literal credentials in workflows.
rules.why: Secrets belong in a vault, never in version-controlled CI.
